user_query: find tariff-driven cost increases with dollar amounts

proposed_columns.name: tariff_cost_increase_mentioned
proposed_columns.description: Whether the filing mentions tariff-driven cost increases
proposed_columns.extraction_instruction: Look for mentions of 'tariff', 'customs', 'import duties', 'cost increase', 'price rise', or 'supply chain costs' in the context of financial impact

proposed_columns.name: tariff_cost_amount
proposed_columns.description: Dollar amount of tariff-driven cost increase, if specified
proposed_columns.extraction_instruction: Extract any explicit dollar amount (e.g., $1M, CAD 500K) associated with tariff-related cost increases

proposed_columns.name: currency_type
proposed_columns.description: Currency used for the tariff cost amount (e.g., CAD, USD)
proposed_columns.extraction_instruction: Identify the currency type (e.g., CAD, USD, EUR) associated with the tariff cost amount

proposed_columns.name: cost_period
proposed_columns.description: Time period associated with the cost increase (e.g., 2024, Q1 2025)
proposed_columns.extraction_instruction: Extract the time period (e.g., '2024', 'Q1 2025', 'fiscal year 2023') when the tariff cost increase occurred or is expected

proposed_columns.name: evidence_quote
proposed_columns.description: Verbatim quote from the filing supporting the extraction
proposed_columns.extraction_instruction: Capture the exact text from the filing that supports the presence of a tariff-driven cost increase

proposed_columns.name: not_found_reason
proposed_columns.description: Reason for absence of data (e.g., 'no mention of tariffs', 'no dollar amount provided')
proposed_columns.extraction_instruction: If no evidence is found, record the reason (e.g., 'no mention of tariffs', 'no dollar amount provided')

user_feedback: It's a decent **first iteration**, but it has three structural problems that will cost you analytically before you scale this to the full corpus. Here is the complete critique:

user_query: find tariff-driven cost increases with dollar amounts

proposed_columns.name: tariff_mentioned
proposed_columns.description: Whether the filing explicitly mentions tariffs as a cost driver
proposed_columns.extraction_instruction: Look for explicit mentions of 'tariff', 'tariffs', 'customs duty', or 'import duty' in the context of cost increases

proposed_columns.name: cost_increase_amount
proposed_columns.description: Dollar amount of cost increase attributed to tariffs
proposed_columns.extraction_instruction: Identify verbatim dollar amounts (e.g., '$5M', 'CAD 2.5 million') directly linked to tariff-related cost increases

proposed_columns.name: cost_increase_period
proposed_columns.description: Time period over which the cost increase occurred
proposed_columns.extraction_instruction: Extract the time period (e.g., '2023 fiscal year', 'Q1 2024') associated with the tariff-driven cost increase

proposed_columns.name: cost_increase_scope
proposed_columns.description: Scope of the cost increase (e.g., 'supply chain', 'raw materials')
proposed_columns.extraction_instruction: Identify the specific scope or category of costs impacted by tariffs (e.g., 'raw materials', 'logistics', 'imported goods')

proposed_columns.name: evidence_quote
proposed_columns.description: Verbatim quote from the filing supporting the extraction
proposed_columns.extraction_instruction: Capture the exact text from the filing that supports the extracted information

proposed_columns.name: not_found_reason
proposed_columns.description: Reason for absence of data in the filing
proposed_columns.extraction_instruction: If no information is found, record the reason (e.g., 'no mention of tariffs', 'no dollar amount provided')
